competitive_position.summary: Competitive field is completely unknown. Round 1 RFI submitted 10 Jun 2026, initial quote delivered 25 Jun 2026. Shortlisted vendors will receive Round 2 RFP (date not confirmed). Operating blind: no competitor names, no Round 1 scoring/feedback, no visibility into relative positioning. Sandra's 23 Jun request for cost estimate to be factored as 'additional evaluation criteria' signals price sensitivity and multi-vendor cost comparison. Risk of losing on unknown competitor strength (price, incumbent integration, services) without chance to counter. Wedge is Merlin AI for negotiation automation and spend enrichment, differentiated if buyer values AI and ROI, but competitive narrative not established.

champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole; SF Next Step 14 May 2026 (last Prince contact); SF Next Step 23-24 Jun 2026 (Sandra operational lead)
champion_strength.summary: Champion strength is weak and at risk. Prince Bedell was the initial strategic sponsor (confirmed Zycus in RFP, attended Gartner event, provided operational access), but his engagement cooled after mid-May with no recorded contact since 14 May 2026. Operational ownership shifted to Sandra Hill in June; she is managing the RFI/quote process but shows no evidence of advocacy (no competitive coaching, no executive introduction, no internal navigation). We have no active champion with Economic Buyer access or willingness to push our differentiation. Risk is high: without a champion we are one of several vendors in a price-driven, arms-length procurement bake-off.
champion_strength.champion: Prince Bedell (cooling); Sandra Hill (neutral, no advocacy)

No Avoma calls with C-level or budget-holding executives
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer who will authorize the $110k+ investment and sign the contract has not been identified or engaged. All recorded interactions are with Sandra Hill (Senior Category Manager) and Prince Bedell (Head of Procurement CoE), both mid-level operational recommenders without budget authority for a six-figure enterprise software deal. Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd is a publicly traded, multi-billion-dollar cruise operator; a CLM+Spend+AI transformation of this scope would require C-level approval, most likely the Chief Supply Chain Officer or CFO. The Next Step log (March-June 2026) contains no reference to executive briefings, sponsor engagement, or budget-holder meetings. Prince confirmed Zycus would be included in the RFP (24 Apr) and attended the Gartner event (14 May), indicating operational sponsorship, but he has not introduced us to the economic decision-maker or validated budget. Sandra's 23 Jun request for a cost estimate to be factored as 'additional evaluation criteria' signals that price will be compared across vendors at a higher level, yet we have no line of sight to who makes that final call or what their priorities are. This is a critical gap: without Economic Buyer engagement before the shortlist decision, we risk late-stage rejection or a price-driven decision that ignores our differentiation. The highest-leverage move is to ask Prince or Sandra to facilitate an executive briefing with the Chief Supply Chain Officer or CFO within the next 14 days, framed as aligning on strategic objectives and ROI before Round 2.

recommended_moves: Executive connect: the deal owner's manager facilitates briefing with the relevant stakeholder Chain Officer or CFO to align on strategic objectives, validate budget, and co-develop quantified ROI business case (cost avoidance, cycle time, compliance improvement) before shortlist decision. Frame as ensuring executive alignment and maximizing value realization, not as sales pressure. Goal: identify and engage Economic Buyer, surface C-level priorities, and anchor our differentiation at the decision-making layer.

Deal team: re-engage Prince Bedell directly (call or in-person if in Miami/South Florida area) to understand why his engagement dropped after May, confirm his continued strategic sponsorship, ask for competitive intel (who else is in the RFP, our Round 1 RFI score/feedback), and request introduction to the relevant stakeholder Officer or Economic Buyer. If Prince is unresponsive, escalate through his LinkedIn/network or ask Alexa Bradley (deal owner's manager) to reach his peer.

Deal team: make Merlin AI negotiation automation and spend enrichment the centerpiece of Round 2 RFP response. Deliver a quantified ROI model showing contract cycle time reduction, supplier price optimization, and compliance improvement (with named CPG/travel customer proof points). Include a live Merlin demo offer. Ask Sandra directly how AI capabilities will be weighted in vendor scoring and what use cases the RFP will test. Position Merlin as the high-value, hard-to-replicate differentiator that justifies our price vs lower-cost competitors.
